records.ety: [OF. cariage luggage, carriage, chariage carriage, cart, baggage, F. charriage, cartage, wagoning, fr. OF. carier, charier, F. charrier, to cart. See Carry.]

defs: That which is carried; burden; baggage.
quotes: David left his carriage in the hand of the keeper of the carriage.
And after those days we took up our carriages and went up to Jerusalem.

defs: The act of carrying, transporting, or conveying.
The price or expense of carrying.
A wheeled vehicle for persons, esp. one designed for elegance and comfort.
A wheeled vehicle carrying a fixed burden, as a gun carriage.
A part of a machine which moves and carries of supports some other moving object or part.
A frame or cage in which something is carried or supported; as, a bell carriage.
The manner of carrying one's self; behavior; bearing; deportment; personal manners.
quotes: His gallant carriage all the rest did grace.

defs: The act or manner of conducting measures or projects; management.
quotes: The passage and whole carriage of this action.
